title: dryopteris wallichiana subsp. bhutanica
intro: Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. bhutanica is a subspecies of the Dryopteris wallichiana fern, native to the Himalayas. It is a medicinal plant that has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
